name: Check N Title Loans
description_short: Check N Title Loans offers vehicle title loans up to $50,000 and cash advances up to $1,500, available online or in-store with same-day funding.
description_long:
Check N Title Loans (CNT) is a Texas-based consumer lender with both physical storefronts and online application options. Customer reviews reference at least one Arlington, Texas location (Abram Street), and the company appears to serve a largely repeat, local clientele. CNT has built a visible track record with a high volume of customer reviews praising staff by name, suggesting an established, community-facing operation rather than a purely digital lender.

CNT offers two core products. Vehicle title loans go up to $50,000 — borrowers use their car title as collateral but keep driving the vehicle while the loan is active. Cash advance (payday-style) loans are available up to $1,500, accessible either online from home or at a storefront. The application is presented as straightforward with minimal paperwork, and same-day cash disbursement is a recurring theme in customer feedback.

Several features distinguish CNT from generic title lenders. Multiple reviewers mention a referral program that rewards existing customers. A zero-interest loan benefit for first-time borrowers or members is referenced in reviews — a meaningful differentiator if it applies broadly. The combination of online and in-person channels gives borrowers flexibility, and the staff culture is notably personal, with customers frequently calling out specific employees by name and returning for multiple loans.

Honestly assessed, CNT fills a clear need for vehicle owners who need fast liquidity and have exhausted cheaper options. However, the website discloses no APR, fee schedule, or repayment terms — a significant transparency gap. Title loans carry real risk: default means losing your vehicle. Cash advances at this scale typically carry triple-digit effective APRs. The zero-interest member benefit mentioned in reviews is unexplained on the main site, leaving eligibility unclear. Borrowers should request full rate disclosures before signing and explore credit union payday alternatives if they qualify.
address: 3500 N Belt Line Rd Ste. #100, Irving, TX 75062

company_info.headquarters: 3500 N Belt Line Rd Ste. #100, Irving, TX 75062

pros: Title loans up to $50,000 — higher ceiling than most storefront title lenders
Cash advances up to $1,500 for smaller, short-term needs
Both online and in-store application channels available
Borrowers keep their vehicle while the title loan is active
Same-day cash disbursement consistently reported in customer reviews
Referral program rewards existing customers who send new borrowers
Zero-interest loan benefit referenced for first-time borrowers or members
cons: No APR, rate, or fee information disclosed on the website
Title loans risk vehicle repossession if the borrower defaults
Zero-interest membership benefit is unexplained — eligibility criteria not published
Cash advance cap of $1,500 is low for mid-size emergencies
Appears to be Texas-only with limited storefront coverage
best_for: Vehicle owners in Texas needing fast cash secured against their car title
Borrowers who need $500–$1,500 quickly and have no bank or credit union option
Repeat borrowers who qualify for CNT's member or referral benefits
Consumers who prefer in-person loan transactions with local staff
diagnosis: Check N Title Loans is best suited for Texas residents who own a vehicle outright and need fast cash — either a large lump sum via title loan or a small advance up to $1,500. The main caveat is a near-total lack of rate transparency: the website discloses no APR or fees, so borrowers must ask directly before committing and should compare against any credit union payday alternative loan they may qualify for.
services: Zero-interest loan for qualifying members or first-time borrowers
In-store loan consultation with named staff
